verses.esv: Paul, an apostle—not from men nor through man, but through Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised him from the dead—
verses.kjv: PAUL, an apostle, (not of men, neither by man, but by Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead;)
verses.bsb: Paul, an apostle—sent not from men nor by man, but by Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised Him from the dead—

verses.esv: and all the brothers who are with me,
verses.kjv: And all the brethren which are with me, unto the churches of Galatia:
verses.bsb: and all the brothers with me,  To the churches of Galatia:

verses.esv: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ,
verses.kjv: Grace be to you and peace from God the Father, and from our Lord Jesus Christ,
verses.bsb: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ,

verses.esv: who gave himself for our sins to deliver us from the present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father,
verses.kjv: Who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present evil world, according to the will of God and our Father:
verses.bsb: who gave Himself for our sins to rescue us from the present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father,

verses.esv: to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
verses.kjv: To whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
verses.bsb: to whom be glory forever and ever. Amen.

verses.esv: I am astonished that you are so quickly deserting him who called you in the grace of Christ and are turning to a different gospel—
verses.kjv: I marvel that ye are so soon removed from him that called you into the grace of Christ unto another gospel:
verses.bsb: I am amazed how quickly you are deserting the One who called you by the grace of Christ and are turning to a different gospel—

verses.esv: not that there is another one, but there are some who trouble you and want to distort the gospel of Christ.
verses.kjv: Which is not another; but there be some that trouble you, and would pervert the gospel of Christ.
verses.bsb: which is not even a gospel. Evidently some people are troubling you and trying to distort the gospel of Christ.

verses.esv: But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach to you a gospel contrary to the one we preached to you, let him be accursed.
verses.kjv: But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him be accursed.
verses.bsb: But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach a gospel contrary to the one we preached to you, let him be under a curse!

verses.esv: As we have said before, so now I say again: If anyone is preaching to you a gospel contrary to the one you received, let him be accursed.
verses.kjv: As we said before, so say I now again, If any man preach any other gospel unto you than that ye have received, let him be accursed.
verses.bsb: As we have said before, so now I say again: If anyone is preaching to you a gospel contrary to the one you received, let him be under a curse!

verses.esv: For am I now seeking the approval of man, or of God? Or am I trying to please man? If I were still trying to please man, I would not be a servant of Christ.
verses.kjv: For do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please men? for if I yet pleased men, I should not be the servant of Christ.
verses.bsb: Am I now seeking the approval of men, or of God? Or am I striving to please men? If I were still trying to please men, I would not be a servant of Christ.

verses.esv: For I would have you know, brothers, that the gospel that was preached by me is not man's gospel.
verses.kjv: But I certify you, brethren, that the gospel which was preached of me is not after man.
verses.bsb: For I certify to you, brothers, that the gospel I preached was not devised by man.

verses.esv: For I did not receive it from any man, nor was I taught it, but I received it through a revelation of Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: For I neither received it of man, neither was I taught it, but by the revelation of Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: I did not receive it from any man, nor was I taught it; rather, I received it by revelation from Jesus Christ.

verses.esv: For you have heard of my former life in Judaism, how I persecuted the church of God violently and tried to destroy it.
verses.kjv: For ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the church of God, and wasted it:
verses.bsb: For you have heard of my former way of life in Judaism, how severely I persecuted the church of God and tried to destroy it.

verses.esv: And I was advancing in Judaism beyond many of my own age among my people, so extremely zealous was I for the traditions of my fathers.
verses.kjv: And profited in the Jews’ religion above many my equals in mine own nation, being more exceedingly zealous of the traditions of my fathers.
verses.bsb: I was advancing in Judaism beyond many of my contemporaries and was extremely zealous for the traditions of my fathers.

verses.esv: But when he who had set me apart before I was born, and who called me by his grace,
verses.kjv: But when it pleased God, who separated me from my mother’s womb, and called me by his grace,
verses.bsb: But when God, who set me apart from my mother’s womb and called me by His grace, was pleased

verses.esv: was pleased to reveal his Son to me, in order that I might preach him among the Gentiles, I did not immediately consult with anyone;
verses.kjv: To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the heathen; immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood:
verses.bsb: to reveal His Son in me so that I might preach Him among the Gentiles, I did not rush to consult with flesh and blood,

verses.esv: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me, but I went away into Arabia, and returned again to Damascus.
verses.kjv: Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia, and returned again unto Damascus.
verses.bsb: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to the apostles who came before me, but I went into Arabia and later returned to Damascus.

verses.esv: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and remained with him fifteen days.
verses.kjv: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, and abode with him fifteen days.
verses.bsb: Only after three years did I go up to Jerusalem to confer with Cephas, and I stayed with him fifteen days.

verses.esv: But I saw none of the other apostles except James the Lord's brother.
verses.kjv: But other of the apostles saw I none, save James the Lord’s brother.
verses.bsb: But I saw none of the other apostles except James, the Lord’s brother.

verses.esv: (In what I am writing to you, before God, I do not lie!)
verses.kjv: Now the things which I write unto you, behold, before God, I lie not.
verses.bsb: I assure you before God that what I am writing to you is no lie.

verses.esv: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
verses.kjv: Afterwards I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia;
verses.bsb: Later I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.

verses.esv: And I was still unknown in person to the churches of Judea that are in Christ.
verses.kjv: And was unknown by face unto the churches of Judæa which were in Christ:
verses.bsb: I was personally unknown, however, to the churches of Judea that are in Christ.

verses.esv: They only were hearing it said, “He who used to persecute us is now preaching the faith he once tried to destroy.”
verses.kjv: But they had heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed.
verses.bsb: They only heard the account: “The man who formerly persecuted us is now preaching the faith he once tried to destroy.”

verses.esv: And they glorified God because of me.
verses.kjv: And they glorified God in me.
verses.bsb: And they glorified God because of me.

verses.esv: Then after fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus along with me.
verses.kjv: THEN fourteen years after I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, and took Titus with me also.
verses.bsb: Fourteen years later I went up again to Jerusalem, accompanied by Barnabas. I took Titus along also.

verses.esv: I went up because of a revelation and set before them (though privately before those who seemed influential) the gospel that I proclaim among the Gentiles, in order to make sure I was not running or had not run in vain.
verses.kjv: And I went up by revelation, and communicated unto them that gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to them which were of reputation, lest by any means I should run, or had run, in vain.
verses.bsb: I went in response to a revelation and set before them the gospel that I preach among the Gentiles. But I spoke privately to those recognized as leaders, for fear that I was running or had already run in vain.

verses.esv: But even Titus, who was with me, was not forced to be circumcised, though he was a Greek.
verses.kjv: But neither Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised:
verses.bsb: Yet not even Titus, who was with me, was compelled to be circumcised, even though he was a Greek.

verses.esv: Yet because of false brothers secretly brought in—who slipped in to spy out our freedom that we have in Christ Jesus, so that they might bring us into slavery—
verses.kjv: And that because of false brethren unawares brought in, who came in privily to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage:
verses.bsb: This issue arose because some false brothers had come in under false pretenses to spy on our freedom in Christ Jesus, in order to enslave us.

verses.esv: to them we did not yield in submission even for a moment, so that the truth of the gospel might be preserved for you.
verses.kjv: To whom we gave place by subjection, no, not for an hour; that the truth of the gospel might continue with you.
verses.bsb: We did not give in to them for a moment, so that the truth of the gospel would remain with you.

verses.esv: And from those who seemed to be influential (what they were makes no difference to me; God shows no partiality)—those, I say, who seemed influential added nothing to me.
verses.kjv: But of these who seemed to be somewhat, (whatsoever they were, it maketh no matter to me: God accepteth no man’s person:) for they who seemed to be somewhat in conference added nothing to me:
verses.bsb: But as for the highly esteemed—whatever they were makes no difference to me; God does not show favoritism—those leaders added nothing to me.

verses.esv: On the contrary, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the gospel to the uncircumcised, just as Peter had been entrusted with the gospel to the circumcised
verses.kjv: But contrariwise, when they saw that the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed unto me, as the gospel of the circumcision was unto Peter;
verses.bsb: On the contrary, they saw that I had been entrusted to preach the gospel to the uncircumcised, just as Peter had been to the circumcised.

verses.esv: (for he who worked through Peter for his apostolic ministry to the circumcised worked also through me for mine to the Gentiles),
verses.kjv: (For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, the same was mighty in me toward the Gentiles:)
verses.bsb: For the One who was at work in Peter’s apostleship to the circumcised was also at work in my apostleship to the Gentiles.

verses.esv: and when James and Cephas and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the grace that was given to me, they gave the right hand of fellowship to Barnabas and me, that we should go to the Gentiles and they to the circumcised.
verses.kjv: And when James, Cephas, and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the grace that was given unto me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of fellowship; that we should go unto the heathen, and they unto the circumcision.
verses.bsb: And recognizing the grace that I had been given, James, Cephas, and John—those reputed to be pillars—gave me and Barnabas the right hand of fellowship, so that we should go to the Gentiles, and they to the circumcised.

verses.esv: Only, they asked us to remember the poor, the very thing I was eager to do.
verses.kjv: Only they would that we should remember the poor; the same which I also was forward to do.
verses.bsb: They only asked us to remember the poor, the very thing I was eager to do.

verses.esv: But when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he stood condemned.
verses.kjv: But when Peter was come to Antioch, I withstood him to the face, because he was to be blamed.
verses.bsb: When Cephas came to Antioch, however, I opposed him to his face, because he stood to be condemned.

verses.esv: For before certain men came from James, he was eating with the Gentiles; but when they came he drew back and separated himself, fearing the circumcision party.
verses.kjv: For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision.
verses.bsb: For before certain men came from James, he used to eat with the Gentiles. But when they arrived, he began to draw back and separate himself, for fear of those in the circumcision group.

verses.esv: And the rest of the Jews acted hypocritically along with him, so that even Barnabas was led astray by their hypocrisy.
verses.kjv: And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation.
verses.bsb: The other Jews joined him in his hypocrisy, so that by their hypocrisy even Barnabas was led astray.

verses.esv: But when I saw that their conduct was not in step with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas before them all, “If you, though a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you force the Gentiles to live like Jews?”
verses.kjv: But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said unto Peter before them all, If thou, being a Jew, livest after the manner of Gentiles, and not as do the Jews, why compellest thou the Gentiles to live as do the Jews?
verses.bsb: When I saw that they were not walking in line with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of them all, “If you, who are a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you compel the Gentiles to live like Jews?”

verses.esv: We ourselves are Jews by birth and not Gentile sinners;
verses.kjv: We who are Jews by nature, and not sinners of the Gentiles,
verses.bsb: We who are Jews by birth and not Gentile “sinners”

verses.esv: yet we know that a person is not justified by works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ, so we also have believed in Christ Jesus, in order to be justified by faith in Christ and not by works of the law, because by works of the law no one will be justified.
verses.kjv: Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified.
verses.bsb: know that a man is not justified by works of the law, but by faith in Jesus Christ. So we, too, have believed in Christ Jesus, that we may be justified by faith in Christ and not by works of the law, because by works of the law no one will be justified.

verses.esv: But if, in our endeavor to be justified in Christ, we too were found to be sinners, is Christ then a servant of sin? Certainly not!
verses.kjv: But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ, we ourselves also are found sinners, is therefore Christ the minister of sin? God forbid.
verses.bsb: But if, while we seek to be justified in Christ, we ourselves are found to be sinners, does that make Christ a minister of sin? Certainly not!

verses.esv: For if I rebuild what I tore down, I prove myself to be a transgressor.
verses.kjv: For if I build again the things which I destroyed, I make myself a transgressor.
verses.bsb: If I rebuild what I have already torn down, I prove myself to be a lawbreaker.

verses.esv: For through the law I died to the law, so that I might live to God.
verses.kjv: For I through the law am dead to the law, that I might live unto God.
verses.bsb: For through the law I died to the law so that I might live to God.

verses.esv: I have been crucified with Christ. It is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me. And the life I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me.
verses.kjv: I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.
verses.bsb: I have been crucified with Christ, and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. The life I live in the body, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave Himself up for me.

verses.esv: I do not nullify the grace of God, for if righteousness were through the law, then Christ died for no purpose.
verses.kjv: I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness come by the law, then Christ is dead in vain.
verses.bsb: I do not set aside the grace of God. For if righteousness comes through the law, Christ died for nothing.

verses.esv: O foolish Galatians! Who has bewitched you? It was before your eyes that Jesus Christ was publicly portrayed as crucified.
verses.kjv: O FOOLISH Galatians, who hath bewitched you, that ye should not obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ hath been evidently set forth, crucified among you?
verses.bsb: O foolish Galatians! Who has bewitched you? Before your very eyes Jesus Christ was clearly portrayed as crucified.

verses.esv: Let me ask you only this: Did you receive the Spirit by works of the law or by hearing with faith?
verses.kjv: This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
verses.bsb: I would like to learn just one thing from you: Did you receive the Spirit by works of the law, or by hearing with faith?

verses.esv: Are you so foolish? Having begun by the Spirit, are you now being perfected by the flesh?
verses.kjv: Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?
verses.bsb: Are you so foolish? After starting in the Spirit, are you now finishing in the flesh?

verses.esv: Did you suffer so many things in vain—if indeed it was in vain?
verses.kjv: Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.
verses.bsb: Have you suffered so much for nothing, if it really was for nothing?

verses.esv: Does he who supplies the Spirit to you and works miracles among you do so by works of the law, or by hearing with faith—
verses.kjv: He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you, doeth he it by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
verses.bsb: Does God lavish His Spirit on you and work miracles among you because you practice the law, or because you hear and believe?

verses.esv: just as Abraham “believed God, and it was counted to him as righteousness”?
verses.kjv: Even as Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.
verses.bsb: So also, “Abraham believed God, and it was credited to him as righteousness.”

verses.esv: Know then that it is those of faith who are the sons of Abraham.
verses.kjv: Know ye therefore that they which are of faith, the same are the children of Abraham.
verses.bsb: Understand, then, that those who have faith are sons of Abraham.

verses.esv: And the Scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, preached the gospel beforehand to Abraham, saying, “In you shall all the nations be blessed.”
verses.kjv: And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the heathen through faith, preached before the gospel unto Abraham, saying, In thee shall all nations be blessed.
verses.bsb: The Scripture foresaw that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, and foretold the gospel to Abraham: “All nations will be blessed through you.”

verses.esv: So then, those who are of faith are blessed along with Abraham, the man of faith.
verses.kjv: So then they which be of faith are blessed with faithful Abraham.
verses.bsb: So those who have faith are blessed along with Abraham, the man of faith.

verses.esv: For all who rely on works of the law are under a curse; for it is written, “Cursed be everyone who does not abide by all things written in the Book of the Law, and do them.”
verses.kjv: For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written, Cursed is every one that continueth not in all things which are written in the book of the law to do them.
verses.bsb: All who rely on works of the law are under a curse. For it is written: “Cursed is everyone who does not continue to do everything written in the Book of the Law.”

verses.esv: Now it is evident that no one is justified before God by the law, for “The righteous shall live by faith.”
verses.kjv: But that no man is justified by the law in the sight of God, it is evident: for, The just shall live by faith.
verses.bsb: Now it is clear that no one is justified before God by the law, because, “The righteous will live by faith.”

verses.esv: But the law is not of faith, rather “The one who does them shall live by them.”
verses.kjv: And the law is not of faith: but, The man that doeth them shall live in them.
verses.bsb: The law, however, is not based on faith; on the contrary, “The man who does these things will live by them.”

verses.esv: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us—for it is written, “Cursed is everyone who is hanged on a tree”—
verses.kjv: Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree:
verses.bsb: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us. For it is written: “Cursed is everyone who is hung on a tree.”

verses.esv: so that in Christ Jesus the blessing of Abraham might come to the Gentiles, so that we might receive the promised Spirit through faith.
verses.kjv: That the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
verses.bsb: He redeemed us in order that the blessing promised to Abraham would come to the Gentiles in Christ Jesus, so that by faith we might receive the promise of the Spirit.

verses.esv: To give a human example, brothers: even with a man-made covenant, no one annuls it or adds to it once it has been ratified.
verses.kjv: Brethren, I speak after the manner of men; Though it be but a man’s covenant, yet if it be confirmed, no man disannulleth, or addeth thereto.
verses.bsb: Brothers, let me put this in human terms. Even a human covenant, once it is ratified, cannot be canceled or amended.

verses.esv: Now the promises were made to Abraham and to his offspring. It does not say, “And to offsprings,” referring to many, but referring to one, “And to your offspring,” who is Christ.
verses.kjv: Now to Abraham and his seed were the promises made. He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.
verses.bsb: The promises were spoken to Abraham and to his seed. The Scripture does not say, “and to seeds,” meaning many, but “and to your seed,” meaning One, who is Christ.

verses.esv: This is what I mean: the law, which came 430 years afterward, does not annul a covenant previously ratified by God, so as to make the promise void.
verses.kjv: And this I say, that the covenant, that was confirmed before of God in Christ, the law, which was four hundred and thirty years after, cannot disannul, that it should make the promise of none effect.
verses.bsb: What I mean is this: The law that came 430 years later does not revoke the covenant previously established by God, so as to nullify the promise.

verses.esv: For if the inheritance comes by the law, it no longer comes by promise; but God gave it to Abraham by a promise.
verses.kjv: For if the inheritance be of the law, it is no more of promise: but God gave it to Abraham by promise.
verses.bsb: For if the inheritance depends on the law, then it no longer depends on a promise; but God freely granted it to Abraham through a promise.

verses.esv: Why then the law? It was added because of transgressions, until the offspring should come to whom the promise had been made, and it was put in place through angels by an intermediary.
verses.kjv: Wherefore then serveth the law? It was added because of transgressions, till the seed should come to whom the promise was made; and it was ordained by angels in the hand of a mediator.
verses.bsb: Why then was the law given? It was added because of transgressions, until the arrival of the seed to whom the promise referred. It was administered through angels by a mediator.

verses.esv: Now an intermediary implies more than one, but God is one.
verses.kjv: Now a mediator is not a mediator of one, but God is one.
verses.bsb: A mediator is unnecessary, however, for only one party; but God is one.

verses.esv: Is the law then contrary to the promises of God? Certainly not! For if a law had been given that could give life, then righteousness would indeed be by the law.
verses.kjv: Is the law then against the promises of God? God forbid: for if there had been a law given which could have given life, verily righteousness should have been by the law.
verses.bsb: Is the law, then, opposed to the promises of God? Certainly not! For if a law had been given that could impart life, then righteousness would certainly have come from the law.

verses.esv: But the Scripture imprisoned everything under sin, so that the promise by faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those who believe.
verses.kjv: But the scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.
verses.bsb: But the Scripture pronounces all things confined by sin, so that by faith in Jesus Christ the promise might be given to those who believe.

verses.esv: Now before faith came, we were held captive under the law, imprisoned until the coming faith would be revealed.
verses.kjv: But before faith came, we were kept under the law, shut up unto the faith which should afterwards be revealed.
verses.bsb: Before this faith came, we were held in custody under the law, locked up until faith should be revealed.

verses.esv: So then, the law was our guardian until Christ came, in order that we might be justified by faith.
verses.kjv: Wherefore the law was our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ, that we might be justified by faith.
verses.bsb: So the law became our guardian to lead us to Christ, that we might be justified by faith.

verses.esv: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a guardian,
verses.kjv: But after that faith is come, we are no longer under a schoolmaster.
verses.bsb: Now that faith has come, we are no longer under a guardian.

verses.esv: for in Christ Jesus you are all sons of God, through faith.
verses.kjv: For ye are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus.
verses.bsb: You are all sons of God through faith in Christ Jesus.

verses.esv: For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have put on Christ.
verses.kjv: For as many of you as have been baptized into Christ have put on Christ.
verses.bsb: For all of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.

verses.esv: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free, there is no male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all one in Christ Jesus.
verses.bsb: There is neither Jew nor Greek, slave nor free, male nor female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.

verses.esv: And if you are Christ's, then you are Abraham's offspring, heirs according to promise.
verses.kjv: And if ye be Christ’s, then are ye Abraham’s seed, and heirs according to the promise.
verses.bsb: And if you belong to Christ, then you are Abraham’s seed and heirs according to the promise.

verses.esv: I mean that the heir, as long as he is a child, is no different from a slave, though he is the owner of everything,
verses.kjv: NOW I say, That the heir, as long as he is a child, differeth nothing from a servant, though he be lord of all;
verses.bsb: What I am saying is that as long as the heir is a child, he is no different from a slave, although he is the owner of everything.

verses.esv: but he is under guardians and managers until the date set by his father.
verses.kjv: But is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father.
verses.bsb: He is subject to guardians and trustees until the date set by his father.

verses.esv: In the same way we also, when we were children, were enslaved to the elementary principles of the world.
verses.kjv: Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elements of the world:
verses.bsb: So also, when we were children, we were enslaved under the basic principles of the world.

verses.esv: But when the fullness of time had come, God sent forth his Son, born of woman, born under the law,
verses.kjv: But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law,
verses.bsb: But when the time had fully come, God sent His Son, born of a woman, born under the law,

verses.esv: to redeem those who were under the law, so that we might receive adoption as sons.
verses.kjv: To redeem them that were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of sons.
verses.bsb: to redeem those under the law, that we might receive our adoption as sons.

verses.esv: And because you are sons, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, “Abba! Father!”
verses.kjv: And because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
verses.bsb: And because you are sons, God sent the Spirit of His Son into our hearts, crying out, “Abba, Father!”

verses.esv: So you are no longer a slave, but a son, and if a son, then an heir through God.
verses.kjv: Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
verses.bsb: So you are no longer a slave, but a son; and since you are a son, you are also an heir through God.

verses.esv: Formerly, when you did not know God, you were enslaved to those that by nature are not gods.
verses.kjv: Howbeit then, when ye knew not God, ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods.
verses.bsb: Formerly, when you did not know God, you were slaves to those who by nature are not gods.

verses.esv: But now that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless elementary principles of the world, whose slaves you want to be once more?
verses.kjv: But now, after that ye have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements, whereunto ye desire again to be in bondage?
verses.bsb: But now that you know God, or rather are known by God, how is it that you are turning back to those weak and worthless principles? Do you wish to be enslaved by them all over again?

verses.esv: You observe days and months and seasons and years!
verses.kjv: Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years.
verses.bsb: You are observing special days and months and seasons and years!

verses.esv: I am afraid I may have labored over you in vain.
verses.kjv: I am afraid of you, lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain.
verses.bsb: I fear for you, that my efforts for you may have been in vain.

verses.esv: Brothers, I entreat you, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You did me no wrong.
verses.kjv: Brethren, I beseech you, be as I am; for I am as ye are: ye have not injured me at all.
verses.bsb: I beg you, brothers, become like me, for I became like you. You have done me no wrong.

verses.esv: You know it was because of a bodily ailment that I preached the gospel to you at first,
verses.kjv: Ye know how through infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you at the first.
verses.bsb: You know that it was because of an illness that I first preached the gospel to you.

verses.esv: and though my condition was a trial to you, you did not scorn or despise me, but received me as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: And my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not, nor rejected; but received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.
verses.bsb: And although my illness was a trial to you, you did not despise or reject me. Instead, you welcomed me as if I were an angel of God, as if I were Christ Jesus Himself.

verses.esv: What then has become of your blessedness? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have gouged out your eyes and given them to me.
verses.kjv: Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record, that, if it had been possible, ye would have plucked out your own eyes, and have given them to me.
verses.bsb: What then has become of your blessing? For I can testify that, if it were possible, you would have torn out your eyes and given them to me.

verses.esv: Have I then become your enemy by telling you the truth?
verses.kjv: Am I therefore become your enemy, because I tell you the truth?
verses.bsb: Have I now become your enemy by telling you the truth?

verses.esv: They make much of you, but for no good purpose. They want to shut you out, that you may make much of them.
verses.kjv: They zealously affect you, but not well; yea, they would exclude you, that ye might affect them.
verses.bsb: Those people are zealous for you, but not in a good way. Instead, they want to isolate you from us, so that you may be zealous for them.

verses.esv: It is always good to be made much of for a good purpose, and not only when I am present with you,
verses.kjv: But it is good to be zealously affected always in a good thing, and not only when I am present with you.
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, it is good to be zealous if it serves a noble purpose—at any time, and not only when I am with you.

verses.esv: my little children, for whom I am again in the anguish of childbirth until Christ is formed in you!
verses.kjv: My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,
verses.bsb: My children, for whom I am again in the pains of childbirth until Christ is formed in you,

verses.esv: I wish I could be present with you now and change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.
verses.kjv: I desire to be present with you now, and to change my voice; for I stand in doubt of you.
verses.bsb: how I wish I could be with you now and change my tone, because I am perplexed about you.

verses.esv: Tell me, you who desire to be under the law, do you not listen to the law?
verses.kjv: Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, do ye not hear the law?
verses.bsb: Tell me, you who want to be under the law, do you not understand what the law says?

verses.esv: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by a slave woman and one by a free woman.
verses.kjv: For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a freewoman.
verses.bsb: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and the other by the free woman.

verses.esv: But the son of the slave was born according to the flesh, while the son of the free woman was born through promise.
verses.kjv: But he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman was by promise.
verses.bsb: His son by the slave woman was born according to the flesh, but his son by the free woman was born through the promise.

verses.esv: Now this may be interpreted allegorically: these women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, bearing children for slavery; she is Hagar.
verses.kjv: Which things are an allegory: for these are the two covenants; the one from the mount Sinai, which gendereth to bondage, which is Agar.
verses.bsb: These things serve as illustrations, for the women represent two covenants. One covenant is from Mount Sinai and bears children into slavery: This is Hagar.

verses.esv: Now Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia; she corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is in slavery with her children.
verses.kjv: For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia, and answereth to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children.
verses.bsb: Now Hagar stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present-day Jerusalem, because she is in slavery with her children.

verses.esv: But the Jerusalem above is free, and she is our mother.
verses.kjv: But Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.
verses.bsb: But the Jerusalem above is free, and she is our mother.

verses.esv: For it is written, “Rejoice, O barren one who does not bear; break forth and cry aloud, you who are not in labor! For the children of the desolate one will be more than those of the one who has a husband.”
verses.kjv: For it is written, Rejoice, thou barren that bearest not; break forth and cry, thou that travailest not: for the desolate hath many more children than she which hath an husband.
verses.bsb: For it is written:  “Rejoice, O barren woman, who bears no children; break forth and cry aloud, you who have never travailed; because more are the children of the desolate woman than of her who has a husband.”

verses.esv: Now you, brothers, like Isaac, are children of promise.
verses.kjv: Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are the children of promise.
verses.bsb: Now you, brothers, like Isaac, are children of promise.

verses.esv: But just as at that time he who was born according to the flesh persecuted him who was born according to the Spirit, so also it is now.
verses.kjv: But as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the Spirit, even so it is now.
verses.bsb: At that time, however, the son born by the flesh persecuted the son born by the Spirit. It is the same now.

verses.esv: But what does the Scripture say? “Cast out the slave woman and her son, for the son of the slave woman shall not inherit with the son of the free woman.”
verses.kjv: Nevertheless what saith the scripture? Cast out the bondwoman and her son: for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman.
verses.bsb: But what does the Scripture say? “Expel the slave woman and her son, for the slave woman’s son will never share in the inheritance with the free woman’s son.”

verses.esv: So, brothers, we are not children of the slave but of the free woman.
verses.kjv: So then, brethren, we are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free.
verses.bsb: Therefore, brothers, we are not children of the slave woman, but of the free woman.

verses.esv: For freedom Christ has set us free; stand firm therefore, and do not submit again to a yoke of slavery.
verses.kjv: STAND fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage.
verses.bsb: It is for freedom that Christ has set us free. Stand firm, then, and do not be encumbered once more by a yoke of slavery.

verses.esv: Look: I, Paul, say to you that if you accept circumcision, Christ will be of no advantage to you.
verses.kjv: Behold, I Paul say unto you, that if ye be circumcised, Christ shall profit you nothing.
verses.bsb: Take notice: I, Paul, tell you that if you let yourselves be circumcised, Christ will be of no value to you at all.

verses.esv: I testify again to every man who accepts circumcision that he is obligated to keep the whole law.
verses.kjv: For I testify again to every man that is circumcised, that he is a debtor to do the whole law.
verses.bsb: Again I testify to every man who gets himself circumcised that he is obligated to obey the whole law.

verses.esv: You are severed from Christ, you who would be justified by the law; you have fallen away from grace.
verses.kjv: Christ is become of no effect unto you, whosoever of you are justified by the law; ye are fallen from grace.
verses.bsb: You who are trying to be justified by the law have been severed from Christ; you have fallen away from grace.

verses.esv: For through the Spirit, by faith, we ourselves eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness.
verses.kjv: For we through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith.
verses.bsb: But by faith we eagerly await through the Spirit the hope of righteousness.

verses.esv: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision counts for anything, but only faith working through love.
verses.kjv: For in Jesus Christ neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision; but faith which worketh by love.
verses.bsb: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision has any value. All that matters is faith, expressed through love.

verses.esv: You were running well. Who hindered you from obeying the truth?
verses.kjv: Ye did run well; who did hinder you that ye should not obey the truth?
verses.bsb: You were running so well. Who has obstructed you from obeying the truth?

verses.esv: This persuasion is not from him who calls you.
verses.kjv: This persuasion cometh not of him that calleth you.
verses.bsb: Such persuasion does not come from the One who calls you.

verses.esv: A little leaven leavens the whole lump.
verses.kjv: A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.
verses.bsb: A little leaven works through the whole batch of dough.

verses.esv: I have confidence in the Lord that you will take no other view, and the one who is troubling you will bear the penalty, whoever he is.
verses.kjv: I have confidence in you through the Lord, that ye will be none otherwise minded: but he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be.
verses.bsb: I am confident in the Lord that you will take no other view. The one who is troubling you will bear the judgment, whoever he may be.

verses.esv: But if I, brothers, still preach circumcision, why am I still being persecuted? In that case the offense of the cross has been removed.
verses.kjv: And I, brethren, if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? then is the offence of the cross ceased.
verses.bsb: Now, brothers, if I am still preaching circumcision, why am I still being persecuted? In that case the offense of the cross has been abolished.

verses.esv: I wish those who unsettle you would emasculate themselves!
verses.kjv: I would they were even cut off which trouble you.
verses.bsb: As for those who are agitating you, I wish they would proceed to emasculate themselves!

verses.esv: For you were called to freedom, brothers. Only do not use your freedom as an opportunity for the flesh, but through love serve one another.
verses.kjv: For, brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.
verses.bsb: For you, brothers, were called to freedom; but do not use your freedom as an opportunity for the flesh. Rather, serve one another in love.

verses.esv: For the whole law is fulfilled in one word: “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
verses.kjv: For all the law is fulfilled in one word, even in this; Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
verses.bsb: The entire law is fulfilled in a single decree: “Love your neighbor as yourself.”

verses.esv: But if you bite and devour one another, watch out that you are not consumed by one another.
verses.kjv: But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.
verses.bsb: But if you keep on biting and devouring one another, watch out, or you will be consumed by one another.

verses.esv: But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.
verses.kjv: This I say then, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh.
verses.bsb: So I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.

verses.esv: For the desires of the flesh are against the Spirit, and the desires of the Spirit are against the flesh, for these are opposed to each other, to keep you from doing the things you want to do.
verses.kjv: For the flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh: and these are contrary the one to the other: so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.
verses.bsb: For the flesh craves what is contrary to the Spirit, and the Spirit what is contrary to the flesh. They are opposed to each other, so that you do not do what you want.

verses.esv: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
verses.kjv: But if ye be led of the Spirit, ye are not under the law.
verses.bsb: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.

verses.esv: Now the works of the flesh are evident: sexual immorality, impurity, sensuality,
verses.kjv: Now the works of the flesh are manifest, which are these; Adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness,
verses.bsb: The acts of the flesh are obvious: sexual immorality, impurity, and debauchery;

verses.esv: idolatry, sorcery, enmity, strife, jealousy, fits of anger, rivalries, dissensions, divisions,
verses.kjv: Idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, strife, seditions, heresies,
verses.bsb: idolatry and sorcery; hatred, discord, jealousy, and rage; rivalries, divisions, factions,

verses.esv: envy, drunkenness, orgies, and things like these. I warn you, as I warned you before, that those who do such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
verses.kjv: Envyings, murders, drunkenness, revellings, and such like: of the which I tell you before, as I have also told you in time past, that they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: and envy; drunkenness, orgies, and the like. I warn you, as I did before, that those who practice such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.

verses.esv: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
verses.kjv: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,
verses.bsb: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,

verses.esv: gentleness, self-control; against such things there is no law.
verses.kjv: Meekness, temperance: against such there is no law.
verses.bsb: gentleness, and self-control. Against such things there is no law.

verses.esv: And those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
verses.kjv: And they that are Christ’s have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts.
verses.bsb: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.

verses.esv: If we live by the Spirit, let us also keep in step with the Spirit.
verses.kjv: If we live in the Spirit, let us also walk in the Spirit.
verses.bsb: Since we live by the Spirit, let us walk in step with the Spirit.

verses.esv: Let us not become conceited, provoking one another, envying one another.
verses.kjv: Let us not be desirous of vain glory, provoking one another, envying one another.
verses.bsb: Let us not become conceited, provoking and envying one another.

verses.esv: Brothers, if anyone is caught in any transgression, you who are spiritual should restore him in a spirit of gentleness. Keep watch on yourself, lest you too be tempted.
verses.kjv: BRETHREN, if a man be overtaken in a fault, ye which are spiritual, restore such an one in the spirit of meekness; considering thyself, lest thou also be tempted.
verses.bsb: Brothers, if someone is caught in a trespass, you who are spiritual should restore him with a spirit of gentleness. But watch yourself, or you also may be tempted.

verses.esv: Bear one another's burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.
verses.kjv: Bear ye one another’s burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ.
verses.bsb: Carry one another’s burdens, and in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ.

verses.esv: For if anyone thinks he is something, when he is nothing, he deceives himself.
verses.kjv: For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself.
verses.bsb: If anyone thinks he is something when he is nothing, he deceives himself.

verses.esv: But let each one test his own work, and then his reason to boast will be in himself alone and not in his neighbor.
verses.kjv: But let every man prove his own work, and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.
verses.bsb: Each one should test his own work. Then he will have reason to boast in himself alone, and not in someone else.

verses.esv: For each will have to bear his own load.
verses.kjv: For every man shall bear his own burden.
verses.bsb: For each one should carry his own load.

verses.esv: Let the one who is taught the word share all good things with the one who teaches.
verses.kjv: Let him that is taught in the word communicate unto him that teacheth in all good things.
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, the one who receives instruction in the word must share in all good things with his instructor.

verses.esv: Do not be deceived: God is not mocked, for whatever one sows, that will he also reap.
verses.kjv: Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.
verses.bsb: Do not be deceived: God is not to be mocked. Whatever a man sows, he will reap in return.

verses.esv: For the one who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption, but the one who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.
verses.kjv: For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.
verses.bsb: The one who sows to please his flesh, from the flesh will reap destruction; but the one who sows to please the Spirit, from the Spirit will reap eternal life.

verses.esv: And let us not grow weary of doing good, for in due season we will reap, if we do not give up.
verses.kjv: And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.
verses.bsb: Let us not grow weary in well-doing, for in due time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.

verses.esv: So then, as we have opportunity, let us do good to everyone, and especially to those who are of the household of faith.
verses.kjv: As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith.
verses.bsb: Therefore, as we have opportunity, let us do good to everyone, and especially to the family of faith.

verses.esv: See with what large letters I am writing to you with my own hand.
verses.kjv: Ye see how large a letter I have written unto you with mine own hand.
verses.bsb: See what large letters I am using to write to you with my own hand!

verses.esv: It is those who want to make a good showing in the flesh who would force you to be circumcised, and only in order that they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.
verses.kjv: As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh, they constrain you to be circumcised; only lest they should suffer persecution for the cross of Christ.
verses.bsb: Those who want to make a good impression outwardly are trying to compel you to be circumcised. They only do this to avoid persecution for the cross of Christ.

verses.esv: For even those who are circumcised do not themselves keep the law, but they desire to have you circumcised that they may boast in your flesh.
verses.kjv: For neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law; but desire to have you circumcised, that they may glory in your flesh.
verses.bsb: For the circumcised do not even keep the law themselves, yet they want you to be circumcised that they may boast in your flesh.

verses.esv: But far be it from me to boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
verses.kjv: But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world.
verses.bsb: But as for me, may I never boast, except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.

verses.esv: For neither circumcision counts for anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.
verses.kjv: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature.
verses.bsb: For neither circumcision nor uncircumcision means anything. What counts is a new creation.

verses.esv: And as for all who walk by this rule, peace and mercy be upon them, and upon the Israel of God.
verses.kjv: And as many as walk according to this rule, peace be on them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.
verses.bsb: Peace and mercy to all who walk by this rule, even to the Israel of God.

verses.esv: From now on let no one cause me trouble, for I bear on my body the marks of Jesus.
verses.kjv: From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.
verses.bsb: From now on let no one cause me trouble, for I bear on my body the marks of Jesus.

verses.esv: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.
verses.kjv: Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen. ¶ Unto the Galatians written from Rome.
verses.bsb: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers.  Amen.
